project.name: One For All
project.description: Unified Enterprise Compliance Platform combining ARIA (ISO/framework compliance), GRID (audit management), BCM (business continuity), and Sentinel (data protection/GDPR) into a single monolithic FastAPI application.
project.goal: Replace four standalone compliance tools with one integrated platform featuring shared authentication, cross-module communication, unified UI shell, and multi-framework support.
project.status: Active development — core modules functional, platform features in progress

tech_stack.frontend: Vanilla JS + Chart.js + custom CSS (no build step)

rendering.aria: Multi-page (each route renders a full HTML page)

template_inheritance: base_shell.html (unified platform shell — sidebar, topbar, theme)
key_patterns: shell_ctx(request, active_module, active_section) injects unified context
_aria_render() helper wraps all ARIA template responses
showPage(name) JS function for SPA navigation
Module sidebars defined in {% block module_sidebar %}

components.name: core
components.description: Shared infrastructure — auth, RBAC, middleware, events, shell context

files.purpose: Password hashing, session CRUD, user authentication
Security headers, CSRF, rate limiting, auth decorators, audit logging
Unified template context builder for the platform shell

components.name: launcher
components.description: Platform core — login, command centre dashboard, admin panel, user management, platform features (workflows, reports, calendar, analytics, task board)
key_routes: GET / — redirect to dashboard or login

components.name: aria
components.description: Framework compliance management — controls tracking, document management, risk register, AI policy generation, gap analysis, Ask ARIA chatbot

components.name: grid
components.description: Audit management — audit lifecycle, control checklists, evidence collection, non-conformances, vendor assessments, remote audit sessions, AI gap analysis

sub_files.purpose: Multi-function AI for audits, risk scoring, parsing
Data access layer for GRID entities

components.name: bcm
components.description: Business continuity — BIA records, recovery plans, incident management (war room), exercises, vendor dependencies, compliance controls, training modules, AI chat

sub_files.purpose: Data access layer for BCM entities

components.name: sentinel
components.description: Data protection / GDPR — ROPA, DPIA, breach management, DSR handling, consent records, vendor assessments, data transfers, security measures, retention policies, privacy notices, training, audit log, AI-powered analysis

sub_files.purpose: Data access layer for Sentinel entities

components.name: evidence
components.description: Shared evidence vault — cross-module evidence storage, file uploads, tagging, linking evidence to controls across ARIA/GRID/BCM

database_schema.migration_strategy: In-code migrations via _run_migrations() in database.py — ALTER TABLE and INSERT statements

platform.purpose: Authentication, authorization, audit trail, cross-module events

shared_features.purpose: Platform-wide features accessible from all modules

aria.purpose: Framework compliance tracking, policy documents, risk register

grid.purpose: Audit lifecycle, evidence, vendor assessments, remote sessions

bcm.purpose: Business impact analysis, recovery plans, incident war room, exercises

sentinel.purpose: GDPR/data protection compliance across all processing activities

data_flows.name: Authentication flow
data_flows.path: Browser → POST /login → core/auth.py → sessions table → cookie set → middleware validates on each request

data_flows.name: RBAC enforcement
data_flows.path: Request → middleware.require_auth → middleware.require_capability → core/rbac.py resolves user roles → grant/deny

data_flows.name: Cross-module events
data_flows.path: Module action → core/events.emit() → events table → registered handlers (on() decorator) → target module processes

data_flows.name: AI generation
data_flows.path: User request → module route → module ai_service.py → config.AI_PROVIDER selection → external API call → response parsed → stored in DB

data_flows.name: Evidence linking
data_flows.path: Upload → evidence_items table → evidence_links table (polymorphic: source_module, source_id, target_module, target_id)

data_flows.name: Template rendering
data_flows.path: Route handler → shell_ctx() → Jinja2 render (base_shell.html → module base.html → page template) → HTML response

data_flows.name: Framework activation
data_flows.path: Admin toggles → POST /admin/api/frameworks/{id}/toggle → aria_frameworks.is_active updated → dashboard filters by is_active=1

external_services.name: Anthropic API
external_services.purpose: Primary AI provider for policy generation, gap analysis, chat

constraints: Single-tenant deployment on Windows laptop — no multi-tenancy
SQLite only — no connection pooling, limited concurrent writes
No build step — all frontend is vanilla JS served directly
Session-based auth with secure cookies (no JWT/OAuth)
All AI calls are synchronous within request handlers
File uploads stored on local filesystem (static/uploads/)
No background task queue — scheduled items rely on cron or manual trigger
Template resolution uses multi-directory Jinja2 loader for module isolation

security.authentication: bcrypt password hashing, 32-byte random session tokens, HttpOnly secure cookies
security.authorization: RBAC with 15 roles and 50+ fine-grained capabilities
security.csrf: Token-based CSRF protection on state-changing forms
security.rate_limiting: IP-based rate limiting on login attempts
security.headers: Security headers middleware (X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, CSP, etc.)
security.audit: All significant actions logged to audit_log table with user, IP, module, action
security.input_validation: Server-side validation on all form inputs and API payloads

architecture_decisions.rationale: Single-tenant Windows deployment, shared SQLite DB, simpler deployment and maintenance for enterprise tool
Zero-config deployment on Windows laptops, WAL mode provides adequate read concurrency for single-tenant use
No build step required, works offline, simpler deployment. SPAs use progressive enhancement within rendered shell.
Enterprise customers may have different API access. Config-driven provider selection with consistent interface.
Consistent navigation and branding while allowing modules to maintain their own UI patterns (multi-page vs SPA)
Direct control over schema, simpler debugging, no migration tool dependency. Trade-off: manual migration management.
Loose coupling between modules while enabling framework sync, evidence sharing, and notification propagation

known_issues.description: The evidence vault page crashes — likely missing template context or DB query issue
Platform-wide features (workflows, reports, calendar) incorrectly placed in module sidebars instead of main command centre

known_issues.issue: Dark theme mismatch on platform pages
known_issues.description: Platform feature pages use dark theme that doesn't match the light shell aesthetic
Workflow engine needs pre-built templates and simpler UX for common compliance workflows

known_issues.issue: Framework management not accessible from main dashboard
known_issues.description: Framework toggle/activation should be on the command centre, not buried in admin panel
Frameworks, documents, and evidence should be shared between modules (ARIA docs as GRID evidence, BCM referencing ARIA policies)
When a framework is activated, controls should be auto-generated via AI based on the framework's requirements

known_issues.issue: Frameworks not pre-populated with control sets
known_issues.description: All 15 supported frameworks need their full control sets pre-loaded (e.g., ISO 27001 has 93 controls in Annex A)
